name: com.unity.xr.openxr.picoxr

1.3.3.name: com.unity.xr.openxr.picoxr
1.3.3.description: Provides display and input support for PICO devices.

readme:
# PICO OpenXR Plugin

- [Release note](https://developer.picoxr.com/document/updates-unity-openxr/)
- [Developer guide](https://developer.picoxr.com/document/unity-openxr/)
- [Download page](https://developer.picoxr.com/resources/)

## Prerequisite
GitHub credentials are needed to install packages from GitHub private repositories. See [the Unity document](https://docs.unity3d.com/Manual/upm-config-https-git.html) for the detail. 

### git-credential-manager (GCM)
#### MacOS
```
brew install --cask git-credential-manager
```

#### Windows
GCM is included with [Git for Windows](https://gitforwindows.org/). During installation you will be asked to select a credential helper, with GCM listed as the default.

## Set your GitHub credential

Configure Git to use GCM by running the following command in a terminal:
```
git config --global credential.helper manager

# If you get "warning: credential.helper has multiple values", you can clear out the existing helpers. Then, try it again.
git config --global --unset-all credential.helper
git config --global credential.helper manager
```

Access the repository one time by using a terminal. For example, run the following command:
```
git ls-remote --heads https://github.com/PsychicVRLab/com.unity.xr.openxr.picoxr HEAD
```


## How to add the package
Click `Window` - `Package Manager` - `+` - `Add package from git URL` and set the git URL.

### Latest version
https://github.com/PsychicVRLab/com.unity.xr.openxr.picoxr.git

### Specific version
https://github.com/PsychicVRLab/com.unity.xr.openxr.picoxr.git#1.2.0
